zh.lifeSummary: 當Brian Macaedo O'Neill, Prince of Tyrone於大約1264年出生在愛爾蘭武爾斯特時，他的父親Aedh Buidhe 'Hugh' O'Neill, King of Ailech35歲，母親Eleanor de Angulo24歲。他和Eleanor Magennis至少有3個兒子。他於1295年登記服兵役。他去世於1354年享年91歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Brian Macaedo O'Neill, Prince of Tyrone于大约1264年出生在爱尔兰武爾斯特时，他的父亲Aedh Buidhe 'Hugh' O'Neill, King of Ailech35岁，母亲Eleanor de Angulo24岁。他和Eleanor Magennis至少有3个儿子。他于1295年登记服兵役。他去世于1354年享年91岁，埋葬在爱尔兰。

nameMeanings.name: O'Neill,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Seamus, Siobhan, Dermot, Liam, Nuala, Bridie, Brigid, Conor, Fintan, John Patrick.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Néill ‘descendant of Niall’, an Irish personal name possibly derived from néal ‘cloud’ (see Neil ).

History: Bearers of this surname mostly claim descent from Niall Noígíallach (Niall of the Nine Hostages), a legendary king of Ireland who flourished in the 5th century. His descendants, the Ui Neill dynasty, dominated Ireland, on and off, from the 6th to the 10th century. The surname itself can be traced with some confidence to one of these descendants, namely Niall Glúndubh mac Aodha (died 919), High King of Ireland from 911 to 919, with a power base in Tyrone, Ulster.

variants.name: O'Neall
meanings: Irish: variant of   O'Neill  .

variants.name: McNelis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, Brendan.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Niallghuis ‘son of Niallghus’, a personal name (intended as complimentary) composed of the personal name Niall (see O'Neill ) + gus ‘vigor’. In Irish the combination of [k] and [n] was generally pronounced [kr], Anglicized as the variants McCreless and McCrillis .

variants.name: O'Neal
meanings: Irish (Longford, Down): variant of   O'Neill  .

variants.name: O'Niel
meanings: Irish: variant of   O'Neill  .

variants.name: Odneal
meanings: Perhaps an irregular variant of Irish   O'Neill  .

variants.name: Nealon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Declan.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Nialláin ‘descendant of Niallán’, a personal name formed from a diminutive of Niall, see O'Neill , Neil .

variants.name: O'Neil
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Conn, Donal, Liam, Siobhan, Aileen, Eamon, Michael Patrick, Seamus, Sean Patrick, William Sean.
Irish (Mayo, Tipperary): variant of   O'Neill  .

variants.name: Neil
meanings: Scottish, English (Lancashire), and Irish (Munster): from the Gaelic personal name Niall (genitive Néill), possibly derived from Irish néal ‘cloud’. In most cases the name probably represents a shortened form of the derived patronymics McNeill or the comparable Irish O'Neill .
English: variant of   Neal  .

variants.name: O'Neel
meanings: Irish: variant of   O'Neill  .

nameMeanings.name: Brian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Irish origin: perhaps from an Old Celtic word meaning ‘high’ or ‘noble’. The name has been perennially popular in Ireland, in particular on account of the fame of Brian Boru (Gaelic Brian Bóroimhe) ( c. 940–1014 ), a warrior who was credited with driving the Vikings from Ireland and who eventually became high king of Ireland. In the Middle Ages it was relatively common in East Anglia, where it was introduced by Breton settlers, and in northern England, where it was introduced by Scandinavians from Ireland. It was quite popular in Yorkshire in the early 16th century, largely because it had long been a family name among the Stapletons, who had Irish connections. They first used it after Sir Gilbert Stapleton married Agnes , the daughter of the great northern baron Sir Brian fitzAlan . In Gaelic Scotland it was at first borne exclusively by members of certain professional families of Irish origin.

variants.name: Bryan
meanings: Variant of   Brian  , influenced by the usual spelling of the associated surname.

nameMeanings.name: Macaedo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Roberto, Fernando, Francisco, Javier, Juan, Pablo, Rafael, Ruben, Sergio.  Portuguese Manoel, Joao, Joaquim, Marcio, Paulo, Afonso, Albano, Caetano, Goncalo, Guilherme, Mateus.
Galician: habitational name from Macedo, a village in Lugoprovince, Galicia (Spain).
Portuguese: topographic name from macedo ‘apple grove’, a derivative of maça ‘apple’.

en.lifeSummary: When Brian Macaedo O'Neill, Prince of Tyrone was born about 1264, in Ulster, Ireland, his father, Aedh Buidhe 'Hugh' O'Neill, King of Ailech, was 35 and his mother, Eleanor de Angulo, was 24. He had at least 3 sons with Eleanor Magennis. He registered for military service in 1295. He died in 1354, at the age of 91, and was buried in Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Brian Macaedo O'Neill, Prince of Tyrone est né environ 1264, à Ulster, Irlande, son père, Aedh Buidhe 'Hugh' O'Neill, King of Ailech, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Eleanor de Angulo, avait 24 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Eleanor Magennis. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1295. Il est décédé en 1354, à l’âge de 91 ans, et a été enterré à Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als Brian Macaedo O'Neill, Prince of Tyrone um 1264 in Ulster, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Aedh Buidhe 'Hugh' O'Neill, King of Ailech 35 und seine Mutter Eleanor de Angulo 24. Er hatte mit Eleanor Magennis mindestens 3 Söhne. Er hat sich 1295 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb 1354 im Alter von 91 Jahren und wurde in Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Brian Macaedo O'Neill, Prince of Tyrone è nato il circa 1264 nel Ulster, Irlanda, il suo padre, Aedh Buidhe 'Hugh' O'Neill, King of Ailech, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor de Angulo, aveva 24 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Eleanor Magennis. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1295. Morì nel 1354, all'età di 91 anni, e fu sepolto a Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: Brian Macaedo O'Neill, Prince of Tyroneが約1264年にアイルランドウルスターで生まれた時，父Aedh Buidhe 'Hugh' O'Neill, King of Ailechは35歳，母Eleanor de Anguloは24歳であった。彼はEleanor Magennisとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。彼は1295年に兵役登録をした。彼は1354年に91歳で死去し，アイルランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Brian Macaedo O'Neill, Prince of Tyrone(이)가 대략 1264년 아일랜드 울스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Aedh Buidhe 'Hugh' O'Neill, King of Ailech(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor de Angulo(은)는 24세였다. 그는 Eleanor Magennis(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1295년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1354년 91세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Brian Macaedo O'Neill, Prince of Tyrone nasceu aproximadamente 1264, em Ulster, Irlanda, seu pai, Aedh Buidhe 'Hugh' O'Neill, King of Ailech, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor de Angulo, tinha 24 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Eleanor Magennis. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1295. Ele faleceu em 1354, com 91 anos, e foi sepultado em Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Brian Macaedo O'Neill, Prince of Tyrone родился около 1264 г. в местности улстир, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Aedh Buidhe 'Hugh' O'Neill, King of Ailech было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor de Angulo, 24. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Eleanor Magennis. В 1295 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер в 1354 г. в возрасте 91 год и был похоронен в местности Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Brian Macaedo O'Neill, Prince of Tyrone nació aproximadamente 1264, en Ulster, Irlanda, su papá, Aedh Buidhe 'Hugh' O'Neill, King of Ailech, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Eleanor de Angulo, tenía 24 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Eleanor Magennis. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1295. Murió en 1354, a la edad de 91 años, y fue sepultado en Irlanda.
